title: Publish gateway self identity before MeshCore BLE requires app identity
description: Why this exists: the MeshCore adapter can project a LICHEN Ed25519 public key through SELF_INFO when CONFIG_LICHEN_APP_IDENTITY is enabled and a self identity has been published, but the gateway does not currently publish a production self identity from a loaded link context before MeshCore BLE starts. Current adapter policy therefore keeps missing identity as an explicit degraded zero-key SELF_INFO so app startup is not broken and no sendable synthetic key is invented. What needs to be done: add a production key publication path from gateway/L2/link identity into lichen_app_identity_set_self(), then decide whether MeshCore BLE product configuration should require/select LICHEN_APP_IDENTITY and add a native_sim gateway-path test proving APP_START returns a nonzero public key after publication while preserving deterministic degraded behavior before publication. Evidence: review found no production call to lichen_app_identity_set_self() or lichen_app_identity_set_self_from_link_ctx(); lichen_l2 exposes only ztest key loading today; gateway init only starts BLE and adapter.
notes:
Implemented gateway MeshCore self identity publication for no-hardware scope. Added lichen_l2_publish_app_identity() to copy the L2 link-context public key into app identity, added gateway_identity_publish_self(), made MeshCore BLE depend on LICHEN_LINK and select LICHEN_APP_IDENTITY, and retried identity publication before MeshCore APP_START dispatch so degraded zero-key SELF_INFO can recover once a link key becomes available.

Tests added/updated: meshcore_gateway_adapter now proves gateway_identity_publish_self() makes APP_START SELF_INFO carry a nonzero deterministic public key and proves retry replaces degraded zero-key SELF_INFO. ping_l2 now exercises the real L2 publication path and verifies app identity public key matches lichen_l2_test_load_key().

Reviews: initial Hume review found P2 fake-only coverage for production L2 publication; fixed with ping_l2 real L2 test. Initial Popper review found P1 MeshCore APP_IDENTITY could be enabled without LICHEN_LINK and P2 one-shot degraded identity; fixed with Kconfig dependency and retry before dispatch. Final independent reviews by Noether and Boyle found no P0/P1/P2 findings; Boyle also ran git diff --cached --check.

Local/static check: git diff --cached --check passed.
